steps.title: Identify the x-intercept from the given point
steps.content: The point $(a, 0)$ lies on the line $y = x + \frac{7}{11}$. Since this point has a $y$-coordinate of 0, it represents the x-intercept—where the line crosses the x-axis. To find the x-intercept, substitute $y = 0$ into the equation.

steps.title: Solve for a by substituting y = 0
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 0$ into the equation:
$$0 = a + \frac{7}{11}$$
Subtract $\frac{7}{11}$ from both sides:
$$a = -\frac{7}{11}$$
Equivalent forms accepted: -7/11, -0.636, -0.637.
